title: CSA CCM v4 to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Security Services Cross-Reference
description: Maps CSA Cloud Controls Matrix v4 domains to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) security services and features that implement or support each domain's control objectives. Use to identify which OCI services satisfy CCM requirements for cloud security assessments.
verificationNote: OCI service mappings are based on publicly documented service capabilities. OCI services evolve frequently -- verify current capabilities against Oracle Cloud documentation. Not endorsed by CSA or Oracle.

ociServices.description: Records API calls and operations across OCI services. Provides immutable audit trail retained for 365 days. Maps to A&A-01 (audit planning) and A&A-02 (independent assessments).
Continuous security monitoring with detectors for configuration drift, threat activity, and compliance violations. Supports A&A-03 (risk-based planning).
Access to OCI compliance certifications (ISO 27001, SOC 2, PCI DSS, FedRAMP, CSA STAR). Supports A&A-05 (information system regulatory mapping).
Managed Web Application Firewall with OWASP Top 10 protection, bot management, and rate limiting. Implements AIS-01 (application security) for web applications.
Automated vulnerability assessment for compute instances and container images. Supports AIS-02 (application security testing).
Managed API gateway with authentication, throttling, and request validation. Supports AIS-04 (secure application design) for API security.
CI/CD pipeline with security scanning integration and artifact management. Supports AIS-02 (security testing in SDLC).

ociServices.service: OCI Block Volume Backups / Boot Volume Backups
ociServices.description: Automated backup service for block volumes with cross-region copy. Supports BCR-01 and BCR-04 (backup and recovery).
Automated disaster recovery orchestration with switchover and failover across regions. Supports BCR-02 (business continuity testing).
Cross-region replication for Object Storage buckets. Supports BCR-03 and BCR-04 (backup integrity).
Availability Domains and Fault Domains within OCI regions provide infrastructure redundancy. Supports BCR-01 (business continuity planning).
Continuous configuration monitoring and drift detection with automated remediation. Implements CCC-03 (configuration management).
Infrastructure as Code using Terraform for controlled, repeatable deployments. Implements CCC-01 (change management) through code-based changes.
Configuration management, patching, and compliance monitoring for compute instances. Supports CCC-03.
Customer-managed encryption key management with CMK creation, rotation, and access policies. FIPS 140-2 Level 3 HSM-backed. Implements EKM-01 through EKM-04.
Dedicated HSM partitions and external key management support (BYOK/HYOK). Implements EKM-02 for highest assurance key management.
TLS certificate provisioning and lifecycle management with private CA. Implements DSP-02 (encryption in transit).

ociServices.service: OCI Default Encryption (Block Volume / Object Storage)
ociServices.description: Service-level encryption at rest using Oracle-managed or customer-managed keys. Implements DSP-01 (encryption at rest).
Unified data security platform for Oracle databases with sensitive data discovery, masking, activity auditing, and security assessment. Implements DSP-01 (data classification).
Compartment-level access control with fine-grained IAM policies. Supports DSP-05 (data access control).
Prevents insecure configurations (e.g., public buckets, unencrypted volumes) through preventive security policies. Implements DSP-04 (data access restriction).
Automated data lifecycle management with retention, archival, and deletion policies. Implements DSP-17 (data retention and deletion).
Immutable retention policies preventing data deletion or modification. Supports DSP-17 compliance.

Datacenter physical security is an Oracle responsibility. Customers verify via SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, and PCI DSS reports covering physical access controls, environmental protections, and equipment security. Maps to DCS-01 through DCS-09.
OCI datacenters comply with SOC 1/2/3, ISO 27001, ISO 27017, PCI DSS, FedRAMP, and CSA STAR — all include physical security audit scope.
Hierarchical compartment structure for resource isolation, governance, and cost management. Implements GRC-01 (governance programme).
Automated preventive security guardrails for compartments preventing insecure configurations. Implements GRC-01 and GRC-03 (policy enforcement).
Centralised security posture management with risk scoring, threat detection, and automated remediation. Supports GRC-02 (risk management).
Core identity and access management with users, groups, dynamic groups, and policy-based access. Implements IAM-01 (access control policy) and IAM-04 (provisioning).
Centralised SSO, MFA, and adaptive authentication with FIDO2 support. Implements IAM-02 (strong authentication) and IAM-03 (MFA).
Analysis of IAM policies to identify overly permissive access. Implements IAM-06 (access reviews) and IAM-09 (least privilege).
API call logging for all IAM operations. Supports IAM-12 (access logging) and LOG-01.
Instance and resource-level identity without long-lived credentials. Implements IAM-10 (credential management).
Virtual private network with subnets, security lists, network security groups, and DRG. Implements IVS-01 (network security) and IVS-06 (segmentation).
Always-on DDoS protection for OCI resources. Implements IVS-04.
Managed network firewall with stateful inspection, IDS/IPS, and URL filtering. Implements IVS-01 and IVS-04.
Intelligent threat detection using ML on audit logs, VCN flow logs, and cloud activity. Implements IVS-09 (threat detection).
Private connectivity to OCI services without internet transit. Implements IVS-06 (network segmentation).
Management event logging across all OCI services with 365-day immutable retention. Implements LOG-01 (logging policy) and LOG-03 (security monitoring).
Centralised log collection and management with custom and service log ingestion. Implements LOG-02 (log centralisation) and LOG-05 (log retention).
Metrics, alarms, and notifications for OCI resources. Implements LOG-03 (monitoring) and LOG-13 (alerting).
Network traffic logging for VCN subnets and VNICs. Implements LOG-01 for network-level visibility.
Immutable retention policies for audit log protection in Object Storage. Implements LOG-05 (log integrity and retention).

mappings.ccmDomainName: Security Incident Management, E-Discovery, & Cloud Forensics

ociServices.description: Centralised security finding aggregation with automated responder recipes for remediation. Supports SEF-01 (incident management).
Threat indicator correlation and investigation support. Supports SEF-02 (incident response) through threat context.
Event-driven automated response to security events. Supports SEF-02 (incident response plan) through automated playbooks.

ociServices.service: Block Volume Snapshots / Object Storage for forensics
ociServices.description: Volume snapshots and object storage for forensic evidence preservation. Supports cloud forensics capability.
Access to OCI compliance documents (SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI DSS, CSA STAR). Supports STA-05 (third-party audit) for vendor assessment.
Vetted third-party software marketplace. Supports STA-01 (vendor assessment) through Oracle vetting.
Private container registry with image signing and scanning. Supports STA-06 (supply chain security) for container images.
Automated vulnerability assessment for compute instances and container images. Implements TVM-01 (vulnerability management) and TVM-02 (vulnerability scanning).
ML-based threat detection across audit logs, VCN flow logs, and cloud activity. Implements TVM-06 (threat intelligence).
Automated OS and application patching for compute instances. Implements TVM-01 (vulnerability remediation).
Container image vulnerability scanning in OCI Container Registry. Implements TVM-04 (container security).
Risk-based authentication with device trust evaluation and network context. Supports UEM controls through conditional access policies.
Managed virtual desktop infrastructure on OCI. Supports UEM by providing controlled cloud endpoint environments.
